purpose: Formal conformance substrate for the three-state trail_status lifecycle: PENDING → COMMITTED → FAILED. Each vector is a complete trail_record. An implementer who correctly models all three states and their tx_hash invariants has a conformant implementation of the guarantee model.

lifecycle_invariants.PENDING: Transitional. Execution started, outcome unknown. tx_hash is null (signer not yet recovered verification handle). A verifier MUST NOT treat PENDING as success or failure.
lifecycle_invariants.COMMITTED: Terminal. Execution completed and on-chain anchor exists. tx_hash is non-null. A verifier who finds COMMITTED + matching tx_hash can assert the action ran exactly once and the outcome is tamper-evident.
lifecycle_invariants.FAILED: Terminal. Execution did not complete OR post-execution receipt never arrived within TTL. tx_hash is null. Distinct from PENDING/null: FAILED is terminal (no recovery possible). A verifier can assert no tamper-evident outcome record exists.

vectors.description: Initial state. agent submitted delegation.execute — execution started but signer has not yet recovered a verification reference. tx_hash is null. This record is honest about not-knowing: execution may have succeeded or failed externally.

Terminal state — success. Execution completed and on-chain anchor confirmed. tx_hash is non-null. A verifier who finds this record AND the corresponding on-chain anchor can assert tamper-evident outcome. Note: different timestamp from PENDING — each action_ref is unique.

notes.tx_hash: Real Base mainnet anchor (pioneer-agent-001 Oasis payment 2026-04-13). Used as canonical tx_hash reference across fixtures.

vectors.description: Terminal state — failure. Execution did not complete OR post-execution receipt did not arrive within TTL. tx_hash is null. This is distinct from PENDING/null: FAILED is terminal, no further transitions possible. A verifier can assert no tamper-evident outcome exists for this action_ref.
